nativeRange.habitat: Hard substrate areas in moderately turbulent waters; subtidal rocky reef zones

summary: Dragon's Breath is a vivid red marine macroalgae prized for its branching, flame-like form. Generally kept as a display specimen rather than for nutrient export, it tends to need moderate blue-spectrum lighting to hold its color.
careNotes:
Dragon's Breath (Halymenia durvillei) is an ornamental red macroalgae known for its intensely colored, branching thallus that resembles dancing flames — the origin of its trade name. It tends to grow either free-floating or attached to rock; attached specimens have been reported to reach over 30 cm in height. The species is native to the Indo-Pacific, where it occurs across the photic zone at depths of roughly 1 to 30 metres.

In captivity it is generally considered an intermediate-care species because maintaining its vivid red-to-pink coloration requires attention to lighting. Moderate blue-spectrum light is typically advised; high-intensity lighting has been observed to cause browning. Iron and iodine supplementation may support coloration in some systems.

Dragon's Breath is considered reef-safe but has been noted as a palatable food source for herbivorous fish — tangs, rabbitfish, and sea urchins in particular — so it is generally advised to keep it away from dedicated herbivores. Propagation is primarily by fragmentation.

The trade name 'Super Saiyan Dragon's Breath' refers to the same species.

Attachment: as a red alga, Dragon's Breath will MELT at the base if buried in sand. It should be attached to rock instead, using superglue, zip-tie or fishing line, or by wedging it into a crevice.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros
additional.notes: Confirmed species ID (Halymenia durvillei); minimal husbandry data — described as very sensitive to shipping, flame-like fluorescent orange tips.

additional.name: Top Shelf Aquatics
additional.notes: Confirmed species as red macroalgae for nutrient control and display; noted herbivore palatability. Minimal parameter data.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
